title: Test Quesadillas
description: Easy, quick, and tasty veggie quesadillas loaded with a spicy bean and pepper filling, layered with melted cheddar cheese.

ingredients.name: Cooking oil
ingredients.note: Plus a little extra for greasing the oven trays.

ingredients.name: Kidney beans
ingredients.note: Two standard 400 g tins — drain and rinse before use.

ingredients.name: Sweetcorn
ingredients.note: One standard tin — drain before use.

ingredients.name: Small tortilla wraps
ingredients.note: About 16 cm diameter. Three wraps per quesadilla.

ingredients.name: Grated cheddar cheese
ingredients.note: Pre-grated saves time. Try mixing in monterey jack or gruyère for variety.

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.note: For seasoning. Smoked paprika works well too.

equipment.name: Two oven trays
equipment.note: Also called sheet pans. Each tray should fit 3 quesadillas.

equipment.name: Large pan
equipment.note: A wide frying pan or sauté pan for the filling.

equipment.name: Tin opener
equipment.note: For the tomatoes, beans, and sweetcorn.

equipment.name: Colander
equipment.note: For draining the beans and sweetcorn.

stages.label: Cooking
stages.description: Cook the filling on the hob, then assemble and bake the quesadillas.

steps.instruction: Add the chopped red peppers to the pan. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally.
steps.hint: You want them softened but still with a bit of bite.

steps.instruction: Add the {1} chopped tomatoes, {2} drained kidney beans, {3} drained sweetcorn, and {4} hot chilli powder (or to taste). Stir to combine, bring to a boil, then leave uncovered to thicken.
steps.hint: Leaving the lid off lets moisture escape so the filling thickens — you don't want it too wet or the tortillas will go soggy.

steps.instruction: Take the pan off the heat. The filling is ready for assembly.

Lightly oil the two oven trays. For each quesadilla (6 total, 3 per tray): place one tortilla down, spread on a layer of bean mixture, add cheese, place a second tortilla, spread more bean mixture, add more cheese, place a third tortilla on top, and finish with a sprinkle of cheese. You'll use {1} tortillas and {2} cheese in total.
steps.hint: Each quesadilla is a triple-decker: tortilla → filling → cheese → tortilla → filling → cheese → tortilla → cheese on top.

triple-decker.title: Layering order
triple-decker.text: Three tortillas with filling and cheese between each layer, plus cheese on top to get a golden crust.

steps.instruction: Place both trays in the preheated oven.
steps.hint: The cheese on top should be melted and golden. Keep an eye on them — ovens vary.

steps.instruction: Remove the trays from the oven. Serve with sour cream, a sprinkle of paprika, and fresh coriander.
